title: change error comparison for exec.ErrNotFound
body:
it seeems exec.ErrNotFound does not work with simple equality checks and
needs errors.Is() to work correctly.

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.3/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I really hate to say it, but our continued use of `github.com/pkg/errors` is going to begin to become a liability as people continue to adopt standard library errors.

I recall @vrothberg linking a library that allowed both `Cause()` and `Is()` to be used, which could be a good option. Still going to be incredibly painful since we basically would have to migrate *everything* to the new error library...

@mheon I think we should use `errors.Is` instead of `github.com/pkg/errors.Cause` everywhere. Errors created with `github.com/pkg/errors` implement both the cause and unwrap method so `errors.Is` should always work.

> @mheon I think we should use `errors.Is` instead of `github.com/pkg/errors.Cause` everywhere. Errors created with `github.com/pkg/errors` implement both the cause and unwrap method so `errors.Is` should always work.

Yes; there isn’t much of a reason to add any new `errors.Cause` uses when `errors.{As,Is}` matches those cases and more. And `fmt.Errorf` is about 3x faster than `errors.Wrapf` because `errors.Wrapf` also records a stack trace. (Alternatively, there’s `pkg/errors.WithMessage` which is more efficient than `errors.Wrapf`.)

OTOH it’s not _obviously correct_ to mass-replace `errors.Cause`, because `errors.Cause` only performs one level of unwrapping, while `errors.{As,Is}` search arbitrarily deep; so that can be a semantic change. (And as long as `errors.Cause` continues to be used by callers, it is also not safe to replace `errors.Wrapf` with `fmt.Errorf("%w")`, because `errors.Cause` can not unwrap the `%w`.)

So for c/image I’m leaning towards using the Go native features in new code, and leaving existing code mostly alone, at least if it is not modified for other reasons.
On errors.Is, I say we start bubbling up the base libraries containers/storage, image and common.  Is 4.0 an opportunity to make the switch?

c/storage and c/image have their own major versions, so Podman 4.0 does not directly matter. OTOH I don’t think we frequently _document_ that `errors.Cause` is going to do anything specific for returned errors, so strictly speaking we can change things any time.

In practice there are about a dozen `errors.Cause` calls in c/storage and c/image, and a bit more in c/common, that would be easy enough to audit or immediately replace; it’s the ~200 calls in Podman that are a larger effort, and could be broken by a dependency starting to use `%w`.
+1 on using `errors.Is()`, especially when looking for errors outside our immediate stack (e.g., non containers dependencies, stdlib, etc.).
